headline: OpenAI builds first chip with Broadcom
detail: OpenAI unveiling its own chip in a Broadcom partnership confirms massive AI infrastructure demand — good for AVGO near-term revenue. But it also signals OpenAI is moving toward owning more of its stack, which could eventually reduce dependence on third-party silicon and pressure AVGO's long-term pricing power. Watch how the market interprets this for your already-stressed AVGO position.

headline: Tech rebounds after global AI selloff
detail: Chip and tech names are recovering today after a broad AI-driven selloff hammered the sector. This is directly relevant to your book — SMH, CRWD, PLTR, and AVGO all took hits and a sustained rebound would help stabilize your margin exposure. The key question is whether this bounce has real conviction or is just a dead-cat move.

headline: SK Hynix plans $29B Nasdaq listing by July 10
detail: A major chipmaker raising $29 billion on Nasdaq is a significant capital markets event for the semiconductor sector. It could create supply pressure on chip-related names like SMH and AVGO as funds rotate or make room. Worth monitoring into the listing date given your heavy chip concentration.
